title: fix(registry): log missing conversion rates instead of throwing
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Problem

`convertUsage` in `shared/registry/registry.ts` throws when a model reports a usage type that has no rate in its cost block (e.g. `promptCachedTokens` for any of the ~17 text models + 3 nanobanana image models that don't list it).

That throw escapes the `c.executionCtx.waitUntil(...)` IIFE in `gen.pollinations.ai/src/middleware/track.ts:170-303`, which **silently drops the entire tracking event before it reaches Tinybird** â€” a billing leak. The user still gets HTTP 200 (the throw runs after the response), but:

- No row is written to `generation_event` (Tinybird)
- No pollen balance is deducted

I verified this against 15 distinct `requestId`s captured from production wrangler-tail logs over a few minutes â€” zero of them are present in `generation_event`.

## Change

- Replace the throw with a `console.warn` that includes `model`, `usageType`, and `amount` so we can identify which (model, usageType) pairs need to be added to the registry.
- Bill that line as 0 so the rest of the tracking event still ships.

After this lands, the warning logs will tell us exactly which models are returning cached tokens (or other unexpected usage types), and follow-up PRs can add the right `promptCachedTokens` rates to those registry entries.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx vitest run` â€” 223/223 passing
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` â€” clean
- [x] Once deployed, monitor wrangler tail for `[registry] Missing conversion rate` warnings and capture the model names that surface.

gist.headline: Patching the pollen leak
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky bug where missing conversion rates were silently dropping tracking events. Now we log the missing rates and keep the data flowing.
gist.summary: Replaced a hard throw with a warning in the registry's usage conversion to prevent missing cost rates (like promptCachedTokens) from silently dropping Tinybird tracking events.
gist.impact: Tracking events now successfully write to Tinybird even if a model reports an unmapped usage type, preventing billing leaks.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a leaking data pipe to ensure pollen/coins flow correctly, representing the fix to the billing leak.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, using a wrench to fix a glowing lime green (#ecf874) data pipe. Small golden pollen coins flow safely through the transparent pipe. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
